mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of S&P 500 cash flows and generating positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >60% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in real retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer demand and residential investment, which compresses aggregate revenue growth and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline in retail sales and housing starts fails to predict negative returns, with a positive return frequency exceeding 50% in triggered samples.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which signals improving economic momentum and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the immediate 5-trading-day horizon during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: Industrial production increases are followed by negative 5-day returns in >55% of observations during recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 210.0, elevated inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 210.0 are associated with positive 20-day returns in >60% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, which supports wage growth and consumer expenditure, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative forward returns in >55% of triggered months, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding aggregate economic activity and corporate earnings potential, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth periods produce negative 20-day returns in >50% of cases, or the correlation between GDP growth and forward returns is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals a contraction in manufacturing activity and weakening business investment, which reduces near-term earnings expectations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in >55% of instances where industrial production falls by more than 0.5%.
